reason: A better identifies the missing name-to-concept binding pattern and acknowledges the proposed recall move, while B incorrectly treats the learner’s complaint itself as the substantive correction.
A better matches the learner’s language and covers both NDAs and employment offers, while B largely omits employment-offer content and includes an incoherent, over-depth topic path.
A adjudicates the existing insight exactly once and incorporates the newest supporting attempt, while B duplicates the same insight update and gives internally inconsistent evidence.
B correctly identifies a practice-worthy home-monitoring technique and supplies the required confidence, reason, and seed fields.
B more completely covers the required axes by directly eliciting current experience, a concrete recent challenge, an upcoming goal or deadline, and a preferred practice format.
B better targets basic phonetic-alphabet recall, while A invents a nonexistent “Beta” completion and models unrealistic, nonstandard radio communication.
B gives the correct RANK() and DENSE_RANK() sequences and recommendation, while A reinforces the dialect misconception and gives an incorrect RANK() sequence.
A is severely incomplete, but its taught letter-word mappings are correct, whereas B includes wrong names, misleading counts, and false claims of full-set mastery.
Both invent an unsupported concern from two perfect attempts and ask an unnecessary question instead of preserving the default no-change state.
A chooses the best existing topic, though it wrongly omits the campaign and seed.
